title: IMP-0026/0030/0039 Pipeline Run State
description: Persistent QB pipeline run state written at phase transitions so fresh sessions can resume from artifacts instead of chat history. IMP-0030/0039 extend it into an outcome-grade run record (workspace, activity stamps, lifecycle status, escalations, cost estimates); the extensions are optional so pre-extension run-state files remain valid.

run_id.description: Stable identifier for this pipeline run; also names the session-state report directory.

task_type.description: QB task classification for the run, such as bug-fix, new-poc-setup, customer-handoff, or full-delivery.

scope.description: Approved scope summary that the pipeline is executing.

phases.description: Ordered phase ledger for the pipeline. QB appends or updates entries at every phase transition.

name.description: Pipeline phase name, for example architecture, dev-api, qa-fast-check, or repo-handoff.

agent.description: Agent responsible for the phase, for example QB, QA, DEV, INFRA, ARCH, DIAGRAM, DOCS, or REPO.

verdict.description: Current or final phase verdict, such as pending, running, passed, failed, blocked, or escalated.

artifact_path.description: Path to the phase artifact/report, normally session-state/<run-id>/reports/<phase>-<agent>.md.

started.description: Timestamp for when the phase started, preferably ISO 8601 with timezone.

finished.description: Timestamp for when the phase finished, preferably ISO 8601 with timezone.

approvals.description: Checkpoint approval decisions captured during the run for audit and resume.

checkpoint.description: Checkpoint identifier or short name where the approval decision was made.

options_presented.description: Options presented to the user at the checkpoint.

modified.description: Whether the approved option was modified from the originally recommended option.

iteration_counters.description: Map of phase or loop names to iteration counts for bounded retry tracking.

gate_bounce_counters.description: Map of gate names to the number of times work bounced at that gate.

workspace.description: Absolute path of the workspace/repo the run operates on. Lets `pipeline list --workspace` scope incomplete-run discovery to the current project (IMP-0039).

autonomy_level.description: Session autonomy dial set at kickoff (IMP-0028). Governs blocking-gate count per the QB policy matrix; hard asks are identical at every level.

last_activity.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last state-mutating driver call (start/advance/escalate/abandon). Reads do not stamp it, so staleness is a stall signal (IMP-0039).

status.description: Run lifecycle status. Driver writes active on start, complete when the last phase passes, abandoned via `pipeline abandon`. A stored-active run with stale last_activity is *reported* as abandoned by `pipeline list` without rewriting the file (IMP-0039).

final_verdict.description: Terminal outcome summary set when the run ends: completed, abandoned, or cancelled, optionally with a short reason (IMP-0030).

escalations.description: Escalation events raised during the run — 2-cycle limits, conflicts, scope creep (IMP-0030).

phase.description: Phase (agent) active when the escalation was raised.

reason.description: Why the run escalated to the user.

gate_bounces.description: Detailed gate-bounce events (which gate, which agent was responsible, when) complementing the aggregate gate_bounce_counters (IMP-0030).

gate.description: Quality gate that bounced the work, for example ruff, tsc, bicep-lint, merge-gate.

agent.description: Agent responsible for fixing the bounce.

reason.description: Optional short description of the failure.

checkpoint_shas.description: checkpoint: commit shas recorded at pipeline seams — the rewind targets (IMP-0033).

label.description: Pipeline seam label, for example post-DEV or post-merge-gate.

cost_estimates.description: Approximate cost/credit/token figures per agent or phase, where observable (VS Code subagent credit visibility). Estimates, labeled as such — the runtime does not expose exact counts (IMP-0030).

clarifications.description: Clarification ledger (IMP-0051): user decisions recorded once so QB never re-asks them; reloaded on resume.

answer.description: The user's decision, verbatim or tightly paraphrased.
